title: Home Appliance Repairers
description: Repair, adjust, or install all types of electric or gas household appliances, such as refrigerators, washers, dryers, and ovens.

tasks.text: Bill customers for repair work, and collect payment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe and examine appliances during operation to detect specific malfunctions such as loose parts or leaking fluid.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Talk to customers or refer to work orders to establish the nature of appliance malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Refer to schematic drawings, product manuals, and troubleshooting guides to diagnose and repair problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Trace electrical circuits, following diagrams, and conduct tests with circuit testers and other equipment to locate shorts and grounds.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Replace worn and defective parts such as switches, bearings, transmissions, belts, gears, circuit boards, or defective wiring.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide repair cost estimates, and recommend whether appliance repair or replacement is a better choice.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Disassemble appliances so that problems can be diagnosed and repairs can be made.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Respond to emergency calls for problems such as gas leaks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Service and repair domestic electrical or gas appliances, such as clothes washers, refrigerators, stoves, and dryers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Reassemble units after repairs are made, making adjustments and cleaning and lubricating parts as needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record maintenance and repair work performed on appliances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test and examine gas pipelines and equipment to locate leaks and faulty connections, and to determine the pressure and flow of gas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Light and adjust pilot lights on gas stoves, and examine valves and burners for gas leakage and specified flame.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Instruct customers regarding operation and care of appliances, and provide information such as emergency service numbers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Contact supervisors or offices to receive repair assignments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain stocks of parts used in on-site installation, maintenance, and repair of appliances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Level refrigerators, adjust doors, and connect water lines to water pipes for ice makers and water dispensers, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe and test operation of appliances following installation, and make any initial installation adjustments that are necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set appliance thermostats, and check to ensure that they are functioning properly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install appliances such as refrigerators, washing machines, and stoves.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Level washing machines and connect hoses to water pipes, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and reinstall parts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean, lubricate, and touch up minor defects on newly installed or repaired appliances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conserve, recover, and recycle refrigerants used in cooling systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install gas pipes and water lines to connect appliances to existing gas lines or plumbing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take measurements to determine if appliances will fit in installation locations, performing minor carpentry work when necessary to ensure proper installation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure, cut, and thread pipe, and connect it to feeder lines and equipment or appliances, using rules and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble new or reconditioned appliances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
